senses.definition: An Irish female first name, traditionally pronounced like "EE-fa".
examples: Aoife is starting a new job in Dublin next month.
We named our daughter Aoife to celebrate our Irish roots.
Aoife introduced herself slowly so everyone could catch the pronunciation.

senses.definition: Non-standard spelling of "aioli", a cold, thick sauce made from garlic, eggs, and olive oil, similar to mayonnaise.
examples: The grilled fish was served with aoli on the side.
She made aoli from scratch with plenty of fresh garlic.
I ordered fries with garlic aoli as a dip.

senses.definition: In early Irish history, a large public assembly or fair where people gathered to make laws, honor the dead, and hold games and celebrations.
examples: Clans from across the region gathered at the aonach each year.
The ancient aonach combined legal business with sporting contests.
Stories about the great aonach are still told in local folklore.

senses.definition: Relating to Aonia in ancient Greece or, more often, to the Muses and poetic inspiration.
examples: The poet dreamed of drinking from the Aonian spring of inspiration.
Critics praised the work for its Aonian grace and musical language.
She filled her notebook with Aonian visions of art and music.

senses.definition: Abbreviation used in technical or academic writing, sometimes for "aortic" or for specific phrases in specialist fields.
examples: The scan showed no significant change in the aor diameter.
In this study, aor refers to the aortic root region.
Check the glossary to see how aor is defined in this textbook.

senses.definition: In some languages, a simple past tense form that usually describes a completed action without saying how long it lasted.
examples: In Ancient Greek, the aorist often shows a single, complete action.
Students sometimes confuse the imperfect with the aorist.
The teacher asked us to identify all the aorist forms in the text.

senses.definition: Relating to the aorist tense, or describing an action as a simple whole without focusing on its duration.
examples: The verb is used in an aoristic sense here.
Aoristic aspect presents the action as a complete event.
She wrote a paper on aoristic forms in Slavic languages.

senses.definition: In a way that is related to the aorist tense or treats an action as a simple, complete whole.
examples: The verb is used aoristically in this clause.
The event can be described aoristically or with more detail about its duration.
In this passage, time is presented aoristically rather than as a process.

senses.definition: Plural of "aorist": aorist tense forms in languages that use this tense.
examples: The chart lists the aorists of the most common verbs.
Different Greek dialects form their aorists in different ways.
Students practiced conjugating the aorists aloud.

senses.definition: The main artery that carries blood from the heart to the rest of the body.
examples: The surgeon carefully examined the patient's aorta.
A tear in the aorta can be life-threatening.
The aorta branches into smaller arteries that supply the organs.

senses.definition: Plural of "aorta", used especially in technical or medical writing.
examples: The specimens included several diseased aortae.
Researchers compared the aortae of patients of different ages.
In the study, the aortae showed similar patterns of damage.

senses.definition: Relating to the aorta.
examples: The scan showed aortal enlargement.
Aortal disease can develop slowly over many years.
The doctor monitored aortal pressure during the operation.

senses.definition: Severe narrowing or contraction of the aorta.
examples: The patient was diagnosed with aortarctia in childhood.
Surgery may be required to correct aortarctia.
Untreated aortarctia can lead to high blood pressure and heart problems.

senses.definition: Plural of "aorta".
examples: The study compared the aortas of smokers and non-smokers.
Vets examined the aortas of several large animals.
Damaged aortas can cause serious health issues.

senses.definition: Abnormal widening or dilation of the aorta.
examples: Aortectasia was detected during a routine ultrasound.
Mild aortectasia may simply be monitored over time.
Severe aortectasia can increase the risk of rupture.

senses.definition: Another term for abnormal dilation or stretching of the aorta.
examples: The scan revealed aortectasis of the ascending aorta.
Patients with aortectasis should be followed closely.
Aortectasis may develop slowly over many years.

senses.definition: Relating to the aorta.
examples: The patient underwent aortic surgery last year.
Aortic valve disease can affect blood flow from the heart.
Doctors monitored aortic pressure during the procedure.

senses.definition: Relating to both the aorta and the kidneys.
examples: The surgeon examined the aorticorenal region carefully.
Aorticorenal bypass may improve blood flow to the kidneys.
The scan showed narrowing at the aorticorenal junction.

senses.definition: A disease or abnormal condition of the aorta.
examples: The patient showed signs of aortism on imaging.
Different types of aortism require different treatments.
Untreated aortism may lead to serious complications.

senses.definition: Inflammation of the aorta.
examples: The blood tests supported a diagnosis of aortitis.
Aortitis can be caused by autoimmune disease or infection.
Untreated aortitis may lead to serious damage to the vessel wall.

senses.definition: Plural of "aortitis": cases or types of inflammation of the aorta.
examples: The study reviewed several rare aortitises.
Different aortitises may share similar symptoms.
Researchers classified the aortitises by cause and severity.

senses.definition: Rupture or tearing of the aorta.
examples: The sudden death was caused by aortoclasia.
Aortoclasia is a medical emergency requiring immediate care.
High blood pressure can increase the risk of aortoclasia.

senses.definition: A medical condition or process involving breaking or rupture of the aorta.
examples: The trauma led to aortoclasis and rapid internal bleeding.
Aortoclasis is rarely seen in young, healthy patients.
Doctors discussed possible causes of the sudden aortoclasis.

senses.definition: Relating to aortography, an imaging test of the aorta.
examples: Aortographic studies showed narrowing of the vessel.
The aortographic findings supported the diagnosis.
Aortographic images revealed an aneurysm.

senses.definition: Plural of "aortography": imaging tests that show the aorta using contrast dye and X-rays or similar technology.
examples: The hospital performed several aortographies that day.
Aortographies revealed different types of aortic disease.
Older aortographies were compared with the new scans.

senses.definition: A medical imaging test of the aorta using contrast dye and X‑ray or similar methods.
examples: Aortography was used to locate the blockage.
The doctor recommended aortography to assess the aneurysm.
Modern aortography has become safer and more accurate.

senses.definition: Relating to the aorta and the iliac arteries in the pelvis.
examples: The scan showed aortoiliac narrowing.
Aortoiliac disease can reduce blood flow to the legs.
She underwent aortoiliac bypass surgery.

senses.definition: A calcified deposit or stone in the wall of the aorta.
examples: Imaging revealed an aortolith near the arch.
A single aortolith was found during the examination.
The surgeon removed a large aortolith.

senses.definition: Abnormal softening of the wall of the aorta.
examples: Aortomalacia made the vessel more likely to dilate.
The condition of aortomalacia was confirmed at surgery.
Aortomalacia may be associated with connective tissue disorders.

senses.definition: Softening or weakening of the aorta, similar to aortomalacia.
examples: The report mentioned early aortomalaxis.
Aortomalaxis can lead to progressive dilation.
Researchers are studying the causes of aortomalaxis.

senses.definition: Any disease or abnormal condition affecting the aorta.
examples: The clinic specializes in aortopathy in young adults.
Genetic factors may play a role in aortopathy.
Regular scans are important for patients with aortopathy.

senses.definition: Abnormal downward displacement or sagging of the aorta.
examples: Imaging suggested aortoptosia in the older patient.
Aortoptosia may change how the aorta lies in the chest.
The doctor discussed possible causes of aortoptosia.

senses.definition: Another term for abnormal downward displacement of the aorta.
examples: Aortoptosis was first noted on a routine chest X‑ray.
The surgeon confirmed aortoptosis during the procedure.
Severe aortoptosis can affect nearby organs.

senses.definition: Surgical repair of the aorta by stitching its wall.
examples: The patient underwent aortorrhaphy after the injury.
Aortorrhaphy was performed to close the tear.
Modern aortorrhaphy techniques have improved survival rates.

senses.definition: Hardening or thickening of the wall of the aorta.
examples: Aortosclerosis is common in older adults.
Smoking can contribute to aortosclerosis.
The scan showed advanced aortosclerosis.

senses.definition: Narrowing of the aorta, which can reduce blood flow.
examples: Aortostenosis may cause high blood pressure in the upper body.
Surgery is sometimes needed to correct aortostenosis.
The scan confirmed severe aortostenosis.

senses.definition: Surgical cutting or opening of the aorta.
examples: The operation included an aortotomy to access the valve.
Aortotomy allowed the surgeon to remove the clot.
Care is needed when closing the aortotomy.

senses.definition: Having no sense of smell.
examples: After the infection, he became almost completely aosmic.
Aosmic patients may not notice gas leaks or smoke.
The study compared aosmic and normal participants.

senses.definition: In Māori tradition, the name of a canoe (waka) said to have brought ancestors to New Zealand; also used in some place names.
examples: The story of Aotea is important in local Māori history.
They named the meeting house after Aotea.
Aotea features in several migration legends.

senses.definition: The Māori name for New Zealand.
examples: They moved to Aotearoa to start a new life.
Aotearoa is known for its stunning landscapes and rich Māori culture.
The song celebrates Aotearoa and its people.

senses.definition: Plural of "Aotus", a genus of night monkeys; used mainly in scientific writing.
examples: Researchers observed several Aotes in their natural habitat.
The zoo recently received two young Aotes.
Studies on Aotes have helped us understand nocturnal vision.

senses.definition: A genus of small, nocturnal New World monkeys, also called night monkeys or owl monkeys.
examples: Aotus is active mainly at night.
Scientists often use Aotus in sleep studies.
The eyes of Aotus are large and adapted to low light.

senses.definition: Variant spelling of "aoudad", a wild sheep native to North Africa, with a sandy coat and curved horns.
examples: The hunter spotted an aouad on the rocky slope.
Aouad are well adapted to dry, mountainous regions.
The zoo recently introduced an aouad to the exhibit.

senses.definition: Plural of "aouad".
examples: Several aouads were grazing on the hillside.
The reserve protects local aouads from hunting.
We watched a group of aouads moving across the rocks.

senses.definition: A wild sheep from North Africa, with a sandy‑brown coat and large curved horns, also called Barbary sheep.
examples: An aoudad stood quietly on the cliff edge.
Aoudad have been introduced to some parts of North America.
The guide pointed out an aoudad among the rocks.

senses.definition: Plural of "aoudad".
examples: We saw several aoudads on the mountain slope.
Aoudads can survive in very dry environments.
The park is home to a herd of aoudads.

senses.definition: A name used for a Tuareg subgroup or clan in parts of the Sahara region.
examples: The caravan was led by an Aouellimiden guide.
She is descended from the Aouellimiden.
Historians have written about the role of the Aouellimiden in regional trade.

senses.definition: In parts of the Caucasus and Central Asia, a small fortified village or settlement.
examples: They rode through an isolated aoul in the mountains.
The aoul was built on a steep hillside for protection.
Each aoul had its own local leaders.

senses.definition: Abbreviation for "Associated Press", a major news agency, used especially in news reports.
examples: The story was first reported by AP.
AP photos showed the extent of the damage.
Several news outlets carried the AP article.

senses.definition: In education in the US, abbreviation for "Advanced Placement", a program of college‑level courses and exams in high school.
examples: She is taking three AP classes this year.
He scored well on his AP exams.
The school offers AP biology and AP history.

senses.definition: Abbreviation for "American Psychological Association", a professional body for psychologists in the United States.
examples: The guidelines were published by the APA.
She is a member of the APA.
The APA holds its annual conference in different cities.

senses.definition: Name of a widely used style for citing sources and formatting academic papers, based on rules from the American Psychological Association.
examples: You should format your references in APA.
The teacher asked us to use APA style for the essay.
Many social science journals require APA formatting.
